title: Air Force Awards BlackSky $99 Million for Optical Payload Development
text: Contract Overview: BlackSky Technology received a $99 million contract from the Air Force Research Laboratory in early March 2026 to accelerate the design and development of a large aperture optical payload for space-based Earth imaging and space domain awareness applications. Initial Funding: The initial obligation of $2.1 million uses fiscal year 2026 research and development funds from AFRL to accelerate design of the payload. According to the company, this contract supports development of a low-cost, precision, large aperture optical system. Purpose and Capabilities: The payload is intended to support strategic and tactical defense and intelligence missions through high-cadence, space-based dynamic monitoring. BlackSky demonstrated operational value with a Gen-3 image captured March 8, 2026, over Santiago, Chile's Arturo Merino Benítez International Airport, showing real-time aircraft monitoring capabilities. Company Background: BlackSky Technology (BKSY) specializes in space-based imaging systems and has been developing high-resolution Earth observation capabilities for government and defense applications. This contract represents significant validation of the company's optical imaging technology and strategic importance to U.S. defense infrastructure.
